error.signature: AI tells a tourist in Spain that restaurants serve dinner at 6pm or that siesta means everything closes 1-4pm

verdict.summary: Spanish meal times: breakfast 8-10am (light), almuerzo / lunch 14:00-16:00 (main meal), merienda 17:00-19:00 (snack), cena 21:00-23:00 (dinner). Restaurants typically don't serve dinner before 20:00 — kitchens reopen 19:30-20:30 and serve until 23:00+. Siesta closure (15:00-17:00) affects small shops in some regions but supermarkets, malls, museums, and most urban restaurants stay open. Madrid and Barcelona are most international and accommodate earlier hours.

dead_ends.action: Book a Madrid restaurant for dinner at 18:30
dead_ends.why_fails: Most authentic Spanish restaurants don't open for dinner until 20:00 or 20:30. Booking earlier finds the kitchen closed or only tapas bar service. Tourist-facing places near Plaza Mayor/Sol may open at 18:00 but quality drops.

dead_ends.action: Tell a tourist all of Spain shuts 14:00-17:00 for siesta
dead_ends.why_fails: Siesta-style closure (3hr midday) applies mostly to small independent shops in smaller towns and southern regions. Madrid/Barcelona/Bilbao supermarkets, malls, museums, chains, and most restaurants stay open through midday. Government offices typically 9-14:00, then closed for the day. Lunch hour matters for restaurant timing more than full closure.

dead_ends.action: Treat tapas as appetizers before a 'real meal'
dead_ends.why_fails: Tapas can BE the meal — multiple plates ordered across an evening. Especially in Granada, Almería, parts of Andalucía, tapas come free with drinks. In Madrid/Bilbao, expect to order. Treating them as just-an-appetizer leaves you over-ordered and possibly hungry early.

workarounds.action: Lunch 14:00-16:00 (set menu del día is the great-value option, ~12-18 €). Dinner 21:00-23:00. Smaller cities may shift earlier; tourist zones in Madrid/Barcelona accept 19:30 dinner.

Plan errands around Spanish hours: shopping morning + evening (avoid 14-17 for small shops), big supermarkets / chains all-day, museums often closed Mondays, restaurants open Tue-Sat (Sun lunch only / closed Sun-Mon common).

Tapas: 2-4 small plates per person across an evening, often standing at the bar. In Granada / Almería the 1st tapa is free with drink; in Madrid La Latina district hosts the famous Sunday cañas + tapas circuit.
